encounter.reason: Annual wellness exam, COVID-era telehealth visit
encounter.location: Minnehaha Internal Medicine (patient at home), Minneapolis, MN

vitals.name: systolic blood pressure
vitals.note: average of recent home readings, patient-reported via wrist cuff

vitals.name: diastolic blood pressure
vitals.note: average of recent home readings, patient-reported via wrist cuff

labs.name: LDL cholesterol
labs.note: improved from 138 mg/dL at prior visit

conditions.name: essential hypertension
conditions.note: Well controlled on lisinopril per home BP readings (128–134 systolic range). No change in status or management.

conditions.name: hyperlipidemia
conditions.note: LDL improved from 138 to 124 mg/dL with dietary changes. Still borderline. Statin not yet warranted per shared decision-making.

medications.name: lisinopril 10 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily

medications.name: fish oil 1000 mg oral capsule
medications.sig: Take 1 capsule by mouth once daily

document_references.description: Telemedicine annual wellness visit note documenting video visit format, patient-reported vitals (home BP, HR, weight), review of pre-visit lab results (lipid panel, A1c, CMP), assessment of controlled hypertension and improving hyperlipidemia, preventive care review (colonoscopy 2019 normal, COVID vaccination discussion), and plan to continue current regimen with follow-up in 12 months.

diagnostic_reports.description: CMP drawn at affiliated lab prior to visit. Only creatinine (0.91 mg/dL) and potassium (4.1 mEq/L) are individually reported in the narrative. The full panel was reviewed but individual values for glucose, BUN, sodium, chloride, CO2, calcium, total protein, albumin, bilirubin, alkaline phosphatase, AST, and ALT are not documented.
